reviews.review_text: 10/10 You guys should 100% make a survival game centered under water. Pretty please, I would buy it for myself and all my friends.

Peak survival crafting, an improvement on the first game in every way. Buggy sometimes but in 2026 what game isn't? play with some friends, have lots of fun. 10/10 would recommend. I'd like to add that the gameplay loop in this game is slightly different than the first: instead of having to move bases constantly, to explore and collect tools/story items and avoid the bigger and bigger attacks from the locals. This game rewards making a base in a central location and having kelvin build things and gather materials for you while you're away, and you will be away usually for a pretty good length of time. so set up some walls and storage and go exploring, you'll come home to it all completed for you (except if a construction requires rope or something he cant gather). You'll want to pack up, leave some stuff behind so you can fill up your inventory while you're out and explore heavily. keep your eyes peeled because you can very easily miss tools or important items. The game rewards exploring often
(two pro tips: hold i to bring out your backpack, you can put stuff on it by combining it with your bag in the crafting menu. i have my axe, sword, pistol, meds, canteen, mre and grenades attached to my bag. in combat and in general its much faster to take something off your bag than to do it from the crafting menu. and if you're using a weapon with multiple ammo types, like the shotgun or bow, you can switch between ammo types by looking down at your feet with the weapon out and press R)

Every time I thought I was safe, this game proved me wrong.
Creepy atmosphere, great survival mechanics, and playing with friends makes it even better.

Start game

Find gun, don't use it for three weeks

Send emotional support simpleton to pick up sticks

Chuck spears at knees like every native is white devil Ace Ventura

Use battery-operated golf cart as divinely-sanctioned Killdozer on all things biped and quadriped

Sustain self on less energy drinks and bars than I consume in real life

Complete game on hard survival without engaging with more than 1/5 of the crafting book

Ending makes less sense than my emotional support simpleton being able to craft 40 foot lookout tower for me

10/10, would colonize again

Great graphics and a compelling storyline. I enjoyed playing through the game with a friend—it’s a fantastic game, and the optimization is better than in the first Forest. I bought it and don’t regret it.
If your computer can handle it, it's one of the best games I've ever played; but it's more fun with friends.
long overdue review but this game was quite fun when it released, havent played it since but im aware theyve added a few things and fixed bugs here and there. definitely worth playing
on low graphics i can run it on a lap top, super good game.

came to the island, threathened the local population with gun, build my own settlement, steal and loot local belonging, KILL THEM if they resist. EXPAND 
i do not know what the lore is, i just love raiding local tribe and gunning them down with my friends

Kelvin Kelvin Kelvin... ♥♥♥♥ you

10/10 I love deforestation and making national parks
So far im impressed with this game. Great visuals for a indie dev. Vast island to explore and building is really fun as well. Highly recommend
Virginia nice, kelvin slow, helpful tho, scary monkey men, beat game fun
mid compared to the original forest, only thing better is graphics and combat (cutting off cannibal limbs is satisfying af)

Great game with the homies! story kinda short but the endgame is amazing just to explore and make a huge  settlement. 60 hours in and nowhere near to the point of giving up and picking up a new game to play.

8.5/10 for me!

I am playing this game with two friends of mine and it has been a really great experience. I definitely recommend this game especially if you have friends to play it with. I am highly anticipating Forest now! I can't wait!!!

Sons of the Forest takes everything that the devs learned from the first and steps it up to leagues beyond its predecessor.

You will be hard pressed to find a better optimized game on the entire marketplace than this, but I can only assume that it will still ask you to have at least a decent pc. My specs are attached, but know that I have upgraded my GPU since playing, and even on an inferior graphics card, it ran like butter.

Sons of the Forest plays in much the same way the first did: Forage, learn your environment, build up your base, and once you've got the hang island life, figure out what's going on so you might escape before the real nasty monsters start to show up. On almost every aspect of this, Endnight has improved their product. The crafting and base building are animation-heavy, creating an immersive and satisfying system on both fronts. The enemies are predictably horrifying, but they continue to scale with your playtime, giving you the chance to upgrade yourself and yo defenses before they get out of hand. And, boy, will you upgrade.

Here, I may just lose the series purists. This game is... decidedly more modern than the first. Within the first few days of my first playthrough, I already had a working solar power system setup and at least one gun in my possession. This game will give you more comforts, including transportation for the first time in the series, that will pull back the lever on the tension and difficulty of the first game by a considerable amount. You might like that, you might not, but personally, I didn't mind it.

Sons of the Forest's story is, as with the first game, kind of negligible, but continues the time-honored tradition of revealing surprisingly clean and nice places alongside blood-soaked jungles, and then they do some Resident Evil note finding to explain it all. You get the idea.

I call it an improvement, but it . Either way, if you can catch it on sale, you'll be hard-pressed  find a better deal on the marketplace.

One of the best survival crafters for sure. Even better with friends. Exploration is fun and rewarding, building your base up and defending it is awesome. Combat is a little janky but it's fun regardless. 
10/10 must play
Subnautica 2 devs suggested this game and they were right this game is much better than theirs. I recomend trying lol

best survival game to play with friends
recommmend

I’ve spent over 50 hours in Sons of the Forest, and it’s been an unforgettable experience! The survival mechanics are intense—crafting, exploring, and battling terrifying creatures keeps you constantly on edge. What makes it even better is playing with friends; teamwork, shared strategies, and the unexpected moments create so much fun and laughter. The world feels alive, the horror is thrilling, and every decision matters. From gathering resources to surviving night attacks, every session is unique. If you love survival games with horror elements and multiplayer fun, Sons of the Forest is a must-play!

Honestly, I thought this would be a minor upgrade from the first game, something that expands the story and served as an okay sequel but ultimately wouldn't add too much.

I was proven very wrong. 

I was constantly finding small quality of life changes, or genuine gameplay improvements that made me think that the developers genuinely wanted to make a good game, something that would live up to the first title. This game is just a direct upgrade from the first game in many, many ways. Especially with the killer soundtrack. If you enjoyed the first game, you're going to adore this one.
На разок в коопе, очень хорошо. Красивая и страшная, уга-буги и чупапи муняня бегают
after coming back to the game a year or so later it feels much more refined and an actual good experience
Don't play if you have responsibilties. This game eats your life, but every moment is worth it.
At the start its very brutal but as you progress and know your way around it gets more enjoyable as you can retaliate against the relentless cannibal attacks
The game is amazing just the cannibal with a big mouth needs to be nerfed or i mean easier to get killed
Whether you play with friends or alone , it’s definitely worth buying. The game has a great vibe and is one of the best survival games I’ve played in a while. Worth every penny.
every agonizing hour i have to play this game with my "friend" is another hour of wishing the cannibals would get to him first.
like it! but if mutants didn't go through walls, it would be even better and they should balance the HP.

I was a little dissapointed with this game. While I really like all the visuals, I found myself getting too frustrated and anoyed at what I consider simpel quality of life issues. I built a simple cabin initially to get me going, using a blueprint. That worked just fine. But then when I wanted to expand, i found major issues with dissaembling some items, and was forced to damage some parts that just wouldn't dissemble. I also found myself getting frustrated with the amount of attacks I'd get, very early on in the game. Perhaps some would enjoy this, but I found it not so much challenging, but anoying, which isn't what I want in a game. The bow and and arrow I also found too hard to use. While the Spear gives you a nice visual guideline, and is fun to use, there is no such thing for the bow and arrow, which seems strangly inconsistent.

After the unforgettable nightmare that was The Forest, I had sky-high expectations for Sons Of The Forest, and I’m thrilled to say that Endnight Games didn’t just meet them — they created something even more ambitious, immersive and terrifying. This is survival horror crafted with astonishing attention to detail, where every sunrise feels like a victory and every rustle in the trees could be your last.

What I loved

First, the world itself is absolutely breathtaking. The island is enormous, varied and shockingly beautiful, with dense forests, rushing rivers, snowy mountains and pristine beaches. The seasonal system is a game-changer — watching the landscape transform through spring, summer, autumn and winter isn’t just visual flair, it directly affects your survival. In winter, rivers freeze over, food becomes scarcer and your footsteps crunch loudly in the snow, alerting enemies. It’s a constant, quiet pressure that keeps you on edge.

The building system is a dream for anyone who loves creative freedom. You can chop down trees one by one, carry logs on your shoulder and construct anything from a rustic lean-to to an elaborate fortified compound. The addition of Kelvin, your AI companion, is a stroke of genius. He’s not just a helper — he’s genuinely useful, endlessly endearing and his simple notepad commands make you feel like you’re leading a real team. Giving him tasks like gathering logs or filling log holders while you defend the perimeter creates a wonderful sense of partnership. Virginia, the mysterious three-legged woman, is another highlight, slowly befriending you and adding a huge amount of heart to the horror.

Combat is visceral and weighty. The bow feels punchy, the crafted spears have real impact, and the firearms are satisfying without ever making you feel overpowered. The cannibal and mutant AI is smarter and more unsettling than ever. They probe your defences, retreat when injured, drag away their fallen, and even display rudimentary emotional behaviour. It makes every encounter feel dynamic, unpredictable and genuinely frightening. The caves — dark, twisted, claustrophobic — are some of the most nerve-shredding sequences I’ve experienced in any game. Going in with a flickering torch and a single friend in co-op is an absolute thrill.

The sound design deserves a special mention. The wind howling through the canopy, the distant scream of a mutant, the crunch of leaves underfoot — it’s an audio masterclass that pulls you completely into its world and keeps your pulse hammering.

Any dislikes?

At launch, there were a few rough edges — occasional AI glitches, inventory management that felt a little clunky, and a story that was more cryptic than some would like. However, the developers have been fantastic with updates, polishing systems, adding content and listening to the community. Today, it feels like a confident, complete package. A couple of late-game enemies can feel a bit spongy, but the sheer terror they bring makes up for it.

Verdict

Sons Of The Forest is one of the finest survival horror games you can play right now. It’s gorgeous, deeply atmospheric, and scarily addictive. Whether you’re building a cozy log cabin while snow drifts down, fighting off a midnight raid with a friend, or descending into the darkness with nothing but a lighter and prayers, this game delivers moments you won’t forget. If you have even a passing interest in survival, crafting or horror, do yourself a favour and step into the forest. Just don’t wander too far from the campfire. Highly recommended.

This is one of those games where you need the wiki guide if you want to beat it. The island is massive and its a chore to navigate, you gotta collect a bunch of hidden items deep in caves IN A CERTAIN ORDER, and if you accidentally overlook a spot in the maze-like caves you'll miss it. 

The first 3 hours is insanely hard. In general it was fun for the first 10 hours but after that everything turns into a huge drag and you realize how slow and repetitive everything is.

First one was better. At least it left some mystery like "I wonder what happens when I get down that sinkhole?"

Subnautica 2 told me to refund their terrible game and play this one instead,
they where right its way better.

Spent 4 hours trying to build a bridge over water just for my restarted military helper npc 'kelvin' to not be able to cross its end, I tried to make stairs etc just anything to consistently get him off and on the bridge, but ♥♥♥♥ no the AI movement is broken in this game

I really enjoyed this game, as well as the first. Wish this was in VR also. In terms of survival horror Forest is a goat game imo. If you allow yourself to be immersed in can be really scary and cool. My only criticism, and i suppose its a matter of taste, is i preferred the style of teh map in the first over the 2nd, but thats not to say its bad. The 2nd may be too big and has a lot of areas that dont get utilized due to map navigation, but that may be totally my play style. Someone else might enjoy having lots of obscure out of the way areas. I also preferred the 1st games map as i felt it had more distinct regions, as the 2nd is more a less a mountain. I really hope they make a 3rd Forest. Id also, at 100 hours, replay the 2nd if they offered it in VR.

The game is very unclear on how to properly progress, or what I need to do. Most of the game play feels repetitive and lacks purpose. The controls and crafting mechanisms feel wonky. There was also not much horror aspects to it, the cannibals just look like normal mob monsters from any other game.
Subnautica 2 brought me back to this gem. I've killed so many fish, and nobody can stop me. I'm a total monster, apparently, and damn does it feel good.
Gave it a try since Subnautica 2 Devs gave a recommendation on it. I am not disappointed.

Game felt cheap. Building was a solid improvement. Enemy AI, POI's, the 'story'... All felt rushed and cheap. Ending boss was almost painfully easy to fight. The story it-self falls extremely flat, even for a survival game. The horror aspect was muted. Environment felt bright and bland. Odd items that didnt belong, patchwork locations, nothing except a single small video to even get a glimpse of the story. There where no survival stakes after the first few nights. Overall, not worth the time nor money.
ive been wanting to buy this game ever since release, since its on a MASSIVE discount atm i knew i had to buy this, this game is...absolutely...perfect.
Mass game brother, killing those inbred bastards is awesome

Good game but FOREST 1 IS BETTER.
Overall it is a fun survival game with friends. Clearly the devs wanted to improve the Forest 1 expereince. 
But to achieve that still some more polishing required. 
8.6€ on sale and for that price I think it is good. 

- - - Absolutely confusing and lame story. Game barely gives any clues or explanations during the entire story. 
- - - Big map but empty. Same copy-paste cannibal villages and mostly the same copy-paste caves. Also just a few enemy types. Much less interesting and also much less creepy than Forest 1. 
- - - Entirely missing character audio. 

+ + + Fine graphics. Runs on ultra with no problems on my PC. 
+ + + Fine fight mechanics. 
+ + + Fine building mechanics. 
+ + + Driving the vehicles is fun. 
+ + + Compared to Forest 1 you can find much more items to use.
fantastic survival game with insane building mechanics that take a bit to understand but its realism is fun, not to mention driving is super fun with friends! 100% recommend
Solid. Seems a bit Un Optimized, but if thats not something you care about, its good.
I love the game so much, I love the way they made the AI with kelvin. Great game. (100% WORTH MY TIME AND MONEY.)
Insanely fun to play to with a friend especially with a 4 euro pricetag!
Spent pretty much a whole day building a base, 10/10 game i love building stuff in games

Survival aspect and crafting is cool so far, but gd... 20 arrows in a dude in the [spoiler] starting area cave [/spoiler] and he doesn't fall, in fact he's running around on all fours faster than I can aim on m/k ... I think its kinda silly and it pulled me out of the emersion and ruined the combat early on for me ngl still give it a thumbs up but not sure how committed I am now already this early in.
Absolutely fire. Basically the first game with so much more also the cannibals are a bit harder to fight.
incredible gameplay and story mechanics but much funner with some chaotic friends
This game is really fun. The story is super good. I play this with my brother and dad, i would recommend this game to anyone that loves a story co-op game.
played through the first one and now playing through this one. much larger map, more punishing, more to uncover. golf carts are silly but fun. worth it on sale
its rely good, but getting all of the stuff on new saves is a bitt annoying
Very nice graphics,good gameplay
My pos pc boots up its turbines every time I start this game. I get bent over by naked men. 10/10 would recommend.
Gliding is amazing and the building aspects are really easy to understand.
Fun building and very beautiful. Frames on low graphics range on average of a high of 70 fps, but reach a low of 40 depending on season and location. Some stuttering included
Very fun game, story is bit weird, but personally i enjoy it. Core of fun is building and fighting. Mutants and canibals are fun to fight. Building works interesting and its original.
Its so funny when the hunters become the hunted, like when i eat the cannibals when they hurt kelvin.

One of the best survival games that I have played. A lot more mechanics, equipment and places to explore than it was in original game. Playing the game was very fun experience with friends so even if you will decide to buy this game do this with your friends to get even better experience.

Это просто шедевральное продолжение оригинальной игры, пусть и с нюансом... Во-первых, все игровые механики только улучшились и появились некоторые новые - строительство, исследование(особенно средства передвижения и gps-трекер), борьба с бусурманами, графика, времена года, напарники и пр. Некоторые косяки все же остались; корявый перевод местами, вещи и враги редко, но попадают за текстуры; но ничего критичного нет. Некоторый стрем в начале игры ощущается, особенно в первой пещере, но как освоишься(поймешь поведение мутантов или найдешь арбалет), игра полностью теряет устрашение какое-либо. Многие моменты стали гораздо более дружелюбными к игроку(например, больше не надо искать оленей просто чтобы ягоды собирать). Оптимизация для такой хорошей картинки тоже на уровне: на моем среднем пк на ультрах в разрешении 1080p фпс ни разу ниже 60 не упал. Единственным ощутимым минусом будет сюжет, а именно - нпс... Диалоги, взаимодействие персонажей, все сделано как будто с бюджетом сдачи с жвачки за рубль. Наш персонаж и вовсе косплеит Фримана и ни разу за всю игру не сказал ничего, а в катсценах его действия тоже испанский стыд вызывают. Впрочем, пройдя игру на самой высокой сложности(были некоторые трудности, но игра полностью проходится и без гайдов и гринда), искренне порекомендую всем любителям фореста поиграть и в эту игру, даже без скидки(20$ реально дешево для такой игры, в наше то время)
I have not been into survival crafting games historically. However this game has changed something in me. Very fun to make a base and survive with friends! Major rock and stone energy!

great survival game with a fun mix of building, combat exploration and horror. Perfect with a group of friends. The story is a bit obscure which means you either miss it or have to peice it together by yourself through context clues and written messages

When i heard about the sequel coming out I was super hyped for it, but then as i was playing it the game really dragged with its map. The map is so huge it feels like a chore to go back and explore different caves to gather important items. Overall its a meh game i would recommend buying this game when its on sale.
Building can be weird sometimes, but overall great game.

This game is great. The atmosphere and graphics are just phenomenal. The ways you can build a base and fight the cannibals is great. The storyline of the game is pretty good. Overall 10/10 recommend game.
I opened the game and immediately some naked ppl jumped me and some others beat the shii out of me, also a big naked abomination tried to finger me, 10/10 game

I'm another transplant from Subnautica 2, this game hasn't disappointed. I don't think i'll look back. At first i thought they (Subnautica Developers and players) were joking about this one being better than Subnautica 2 but as i play it they were being serious. Never seen a competitor recommend another game but they have respect from me for letting me know their game isn't as good!!!!
A great game to pass some times and to have fun with your friends.
From the graphics to the building to the interaction with the NPCs. Overall love this game and awesome to play with friends.
this is such a good game to the graphics to the atmosphere me and my friend are enjoying this game def would get if your thinking about it

Game overall is fun, art is great, vibe is great. However I have a gigantic issue with survival games (or really most games) thinking a lack of any autosave is a good choice in any game past 2010. 

For a survival game made in 2024 you think having a night of sleep without being woken up by an ambush would mean itd make a decent point for the game to autosave so hours of progress for the unaware that its manual save only isnt lost. Even games like don't starve together (which imo is even more punishing than this game) HAS AN AUTOSAVE FEATURE ALBIET INCREDIBLY LIMITED. Or better yet project zomboid WILL autosave on a night of rest when you ARENT woken up. Or it will autosave ONCE a day. Crazy how two games that usually center around permadeath (I know theres ways to come back in DST but from my experience 99% of players never reach anything else aside from the world generated locations for a single revive) have autosave but this game which isnt based around a permadeath loop doesnt.

Make sure you use the golf cart and exterminate any cannibal even breathing in your direction early game, they will pull looney toons logic out of their ass to make sure you die. So dont play fair either. 

It can be fun, I wish there was a number rating system instead of this yes or no. Because it feels unfair to put it at a no but also feels unfair to have grievances and have it stuck to yes.

Theres no tension in losing my time idk why any dev thinks thats tension. Its a design crutch. Because no autosave means no protections against crashes either. Which is even a worse way of learning theres no autosave than the way I found out. It just makes me not want to play it as often if I know when I die I can potentially lose hours, and id rather put it in game thats respects my time than waste it because I at least know then through hours of stress I at least have something instead of log walls that will be obliterated within the next few in game days. 

The game is not serious enough that a lack of autosave would be understandable. I am being chased by naked men running on all fours all the time while a deaf guy is gathering rocks and a blonde mutant in a one piece suit is stalking me. There is nothing serious about this game at all.

If I had lost all that progress due to a crash which is stuff that usually isnt the fault of the player id have been a lot more upset. Save yourself time if you're more casual and either get an autosave mod or brute force saves through console commands.
very great and relaxing game with great graphics and scenery
love the game just wish the performance was alittle better im using DLSS on high and its still stuttering

I had gotten this game to play with a fried as I had watched playthroughs of the first and thought this one would be fun to try myself. While the building is very in depth and much better than the first I cannot say the same for the story. We were constantly confused throughout the gameplay trying to find out what the story was, and our confusion was not from a lack of collecting because we had gotten every collectable. It just felt unfinished especially by the end you don't know who anyone around you is, besides Kelvin and Virginia, there was a big downgrade from substance from the last. 
Gameplay was a 9/10 my friend and I had a great time building an extravagant base, collecting everything, and exploring cave. The only thing that knock it down for me was lack of movement for the creative mode. Just don't expect much from the story.

It's like Ark Survival Evolved but with cannibals instead of dinos. Graphics are just beautifuland super smooth which honestly makes 90% of my opinion. But the open world survival is awesome. Spend many irl hours building huge house and then go get the modern axe and clear all the caves. I'm almost done with the story mode but I've really been putting it off. Great game

one of the best sequels ever made when it comes to survival and the story. even if it looks slightly rushed i still cannot deny its amazing ive played the first forest game and i knew i would be that same beast in this game too. thank you endnight games and good luck on Forest 3

Hands down one of the best adventure/horror games I've ever played. Made memories on here with my friends I will never forget. Epic. Gotta be honest - I'm a bit disappointed that the next version will be set in a futuristic environment, I'm still not over how good this environment and mechanics felt.

A fun survival game that is an improvement in every way from its predecessor.

[b]Pros:[/b]
++ Beautiful graphics
++ Mobs have different behaviors and tactics.
++ Innovative building mechanics.
++ Experiences change suddenly--from intense and creepy to fun and at times wacky.
++ Stability.
++ Single player and multiplayer.
++ Difficult to survive and thrive--this game does not hold your hang.

[b]Cons:[/b]
-- Difficult to get started
-- Can be overwhelming at first.

[b]TLDR:[/b]
While it can be overwhelming at first, this game is great game that is beautiful, challenging and innovative. If you like open world, I definitely recommend this!
The progression of the mutants attacking your base is a little crazy but the game is dope.

Crafting is great, love the inventory visuals. 
Playing with controller is smooth. 
Graphics are beautiful, first game I've played that gives you a true feeling of a windy day. 
I play this while listening to Horrific Cave Diving stories. 
Life is Good.
Ive played both forest games but i enjoyed this one far more, it was very clean and finished with variety of weapons and armors that made everything feel unique, , overall a pretty good purchase.
i think the game has some fun machanics and i recamend it cause the building is fun but the store and brightness could be better. great game

Sons of The Forest by Endnight is a fantastic example of the rare sequel that improves on its predecessor in almost every measurable way, from story to mechanics and gameplay. With drastically improved, beautiful rendering, SoTF might have the most convincing Forest (hehe) of any survival sim, or game for that matter, that I've played. This, coupled with the terrifying screams of cannibois and cannibabes, and accurately dark nights, gives an atmosphere that is incomparable. In the mere five hours that I've played thus far -(five of many more to come)- I've been impressed, jumped outta my seat (beware of unwittingly disturbing the cave bats), laughed, and found reason to finally shelf the original Forest. This game is a fantastic win for Endnight, players, and base-construction enthusiasts! btw; $8.99 isn't just a steal, it's capital robbery.

graphics are great, good mechanics, LOVE the realism, and there are many things that you can do in this game. Its even better with friends. GET THIS GAME AS SOON AS POSSIBLE IF YOU WANT A SCARY/REALISTIC MINECRAFT

this is an amazing game highly recommended for people who love the thrill of having an amazing gameplay experience and the constant feeling of being watched

its a good product definitely worth the money along with it getting from 60 to 80 fps with ultra settings and FSR 3 on quality mode it provides a playable frame rate with stunning landscapes and amazing animation
this game is a mastrpiece, building bases w friends while being chased by all kinds of cannibals. And the story is insane. Its a must play.

This game is in the same realm as subnatica. You less you know about it the better game is for you. I don't even like horror that much, but the survival game mechanics are amazing enough to make you forgot the horror until a creature made of legs shows up. I highly recommend this game to anyone who loves survival games and a decent story. It's a massive map and plenty of danger, but you have plenty of options to defend yourself. Download and enjoy!

Absolute blast of a game! It's a little tough when you're first starting out with nothing and building a stick bow for food and protection. But after some exploration and collecting some key items, you'll own the forest, slinging crossbow bolts while covered in creepy armor!

Bring on Forest 3! XD
amazing game got a little confused some parts didnt really need a lot of tutorials so thats good

I loved THE FOREST and this game is such a solid successor. If you like basebuilding, survival horror, mystery, open world, body horror (think David Cronenberg or John Carpenter), then you will find things to love about this game.

Most immersive game by far, gameplay is fun, graphics are beautiful, performance is OK (it demands but it also looks great).

Most players doesn't take from it not even it's 50%, game is FULL of details they just don't see, for example the big guy with the motorboat obliberates most players, if u just shoot the motor it explodes and wipes him out, nobody tries it... Same with the million finger dude, u shoot him in the knee and he drops, completely exposed, yeah sure in low difficulties u don't even need to do this, but in the hardest ones it's amazing to be able to exploit the vast details this indie masterpiece has given us.
Very cool game and story. I would recommend to play The Forest first before playing this.
it,s so realistic and with stupuid monsters

Honestly, breath taking, wonderful graphism, the only game were i actually enjoy sitting on a chair watching the landscape
Kind of the same as the previous one but way way better in pretty much everything. Highly recommend if you like that type of base building/exploration/.. game

Short story if you run through it, but it's a great sequel to The Forest. Building mechanics are great, seasons are better extended and the same if you turn the health depletion and such on max difficulty. Great game. 8/10. Plenty of replayability. Even with a lower tier laptop, it still ran 40-60fps on Ultra settings.

A great game compared to the first one. The survival aspect of it is tough. But i recommend getting a better axe than the one you spawn in with. than the game becomes a bit easier. Also Parrying will be your best friend. Combat is good. Like i said parrying is better than blocking if you time it right and just fighting cannibals are fun as ♥♥♥♥. Aaand story is good. Me and my best friend (named landon) Liked the first one so no surprise i like the second one. so my overall rating 9/10. But play the first one before this.
this game is very fun to play with friends and thrilling going against the monsters
